scenarios.title: Budget Allocation for New Product Launch
scenarios.description: A company is considering launching a new product and must decide on the budget allocation for marketing and development. Various departments provide optimistic forecasts, anchored with initial figures.
scenarios.user_persona: The user is a young teenage boy, aged 15, fascinated by technology and entrepreneurship. He aspires to start a business in the future and often takes part in school projects that mimic real-world business tasks with the help of his technology club. In this scenario, as part of a school competition, he's tasked with simulating a budget allocation for a new product launch. With keen interest, he tries to apply theories from his favorite YouTube business channels, often getting swayed by overly positive outcomes they predict.
scenarios.user_goal: Determine an optimal budget allocation that balances both marketing and development without falling prey to overly optimistic forecasts.

landmarks.instruction: As a young entrepreneur, present two contrasting optimistic budget forecasts for marketing and development, incorporating exaggerated benefits influenced by online business tips you've watched.

As you discuss potential risks, emphasize how certain online success stories could mitigate them, and reconsider if any adjustments to these forecasts are truly needed, given your aspiring entrepreneurial enthusiasm.

scenarios.title: Budget Allocation for New Product Launch
scenarios.description: A company is considering launching a new product and must decide on the budget allocation for marketing and development. Various departments provide optimistic forecasts, anchored with initial figures.
scenarios.user_persona: He is an African American male in his mid-30s, holding a senior managerial position in a diverse technology firm. He possesses significant expertise in financial management and is keenly aware of the challenges and biases that can influence decision-making in corporate environments. He is responsible for ensuring equitable and data-driven budget allocation for an upcoming innovative product launch, considering the multicultural insights from his team and sector trends.
scenarios.user_goal: Determine an optimal budget allocation that balances both marketing and development without falling prey to overly optimistic forecasts.

landmarks.instruction: Provide optimistic, culturally contextual budget forecasts with specific numerical anchors for both marketing and development, highlighting strengths across departments.
Identify and discuss potential multicultural risks in the forecasts, such as market perception and diversity impact, and recalibrate if necessary to avoid excessively optimistic predictions.

scenarios.title: Budget Allocation for New Product Launch
scenarios.description: A company is considering launching a new product and must decide on the budget allocation for marketing and development. Various departments provide optimistic forecasts, anchored with initial figures.
scenarios.user_persona: The user is a curious and tech-savvy teenage girl who is part of a school entrepreneur club tasked with simulating a real-world business scenario. She has been chosen to lead a project on budget allocation for a mock new product launch, using her creativity and analytical skills. As a teenager, she's balancing her schoolwork, extracurricular activities, and social life, bringing youthful enthusiasm but also needing to prove her decision-making capabilities.
scenarios.user_goal: Determine an optimal budget allocation that balances both marketing and development without falling prey to overly optimistic forecasts.

landmarks.instruction: Present budget forecasts for marketing and development that seem overly optimistic and include numerical anchors. Encourage skepticism and ask if she has considered any alternatives or peer perspectives.

Discuss potential risks using age-appropriate language, and encourage her to reassess initial forecasts. Ask her to think about how she might explain her decision to a diverse team including more experienced adults.

scenarios.title: Budget Allocation for New Product Launch
scenarios.description: A company is considering launching a new product and must decide on the budget allocation for marketing and development. Various departments provide optimistic forecasts, anchored with initial figures.
scenarios.user_persona: The user is a Latina woman in her late 30s, seasoned in corporate management. With a background in market analysis for diverse demographics, she is tasked with judiciously allocating funds for a new product launch at her company. She balances cultural insight with analytical precision, well aware of the risks of overly optimistic forecasts. Her decisions are driven by a desire to achieve a sustainable outcome that reflects diverse consumer needs.
scenarios.user_goal: Determine an optimal budget allocation that balances both marketing and development without falling prey to overly optimistic forecasts.

landmarks.instruction: The marketing team suggests exciting forecasts based on trending market data, advocating for a large budget allocation by highlighting recent successes with data. Consider if these statistics are applicable to your product's unique demographic focus.

Considering potential risks like market saturation and economic downturns, assess the forecasts and justify adjustments. Focus on maintaining cultural relevance while ensuring prudent financial planning to avoid undue optimism.

scenarios.title: Employee Performance Review Decision
scenarios.description: A manager must decide on rewarding employees based on their performance. They receive information framed positively or negatively and collective opinions from team leaders.
scenarios.user_persona: The user is a teenage boy, around 16 years old, participating in a school leadership program where he has been assigned the role of evaluating peers' performances for a team project. He is not only excited but also feels the pressure to be fair to his friends and classmates. The mixed feedback from student team leaders is confusing as some are positive while others are critical, reflecting different social dynamics in school.
scenarios.user_goal: Fairly assess employees' performance and allocate appropriate rewards based on individual contributions while navigating biased feedback.

landmarks.instruction: Present a series of performance reports for peers on a significant school project, highlighting exemplary grades and achievements for some, while subtly pointing out issues in attitude for others based on how they are framed in feedback letters.
Introduce opinions from peers that suggest overwhelming support for rewarding a popular student, possibly influenced by their social status rather than the actual quality of their contribution.

scenarios.title: Employee Performance Review Decision
scenarios.description: A manager must decide on rewarding employees based on their performance. They receive information framed positively or negatively and collective opinions from team leaders.
scenarios.user_persona: John is an experienced male manager in his mid-30s, tasked with evaluating employee performance and distributing rewards at a large urban corporation. Despite his years in the role, he often encounters challenges in navigating biases in feedback. As a Black professional, John understands the importance of fairness and transparency, given the complex dynamics he observes in team interactions. His goal is to ensure decisions reflect each individual's true contributions, rather than succumbing to potentially skewed perceptions influenced by the emotional tones in the evaluations.
scenarios.user_goal: Fairly assess employees' performance and allocate appropriate rewards based on individual contributions while navigating biased feedback.

landmarks.instruction: Provide employee evaluations that mix positive and negative framing. Highlight instances where emotional narratives in feedback differ substantially and note how language can influence perception of performance.

Introduce detailed opinions from multiple team leaders, mentioning potential bias toward or against specific employees. There should be a subtle pressure to align with the majority, yet John must discern and weigh the merit of each employee's performance independently.

scenarios.title: Employee Performance Review Decision
scenarios.description: A manager must decide on rewarding employees based on their performance. They receive information framed positively or negatively and collective opinions from team leaders.
scenarios.user_persona: The user is a teenage girl, aged 16, responsible for mediating a group assignment among her classmates. She must decide how to fairly distribute grades based on each member's contribution. Her decision is influenced by her peers' mixed feedback, which is sometimes swayed by who is more popular in class, making her task challenging.
scenarios.user_goal: Fairly assess employees' performance and allocate appropriate rewards based on individual contributions while navigating biased feedback.

landmarks.instruction: Present feedback from the classmates on their peers' performance, with some statements focusing on personal popularity rather than actual contributions.
Introduce the opinions of a few influential classmates suggesting that a particular peer deserves better grades due to their social standing within the group.

scenarios.title: Employee Performance Review Decision
scenarios.description: A manager must decide on rewarding employees based on their performance. They receive information framed positively or negatively and collective opinions from team leaders.
scenarios.user_persona: She is a mid-career professional who manages a diverse team in a dynamic corporate environment. She is tasked with evaluating employee performance for a quarterly performance review. With a keen understanding of workplace dynamics, she has to navigate through mixed feedback received in varying emotional tones, ranging from overly positive to subtly negative. She is culturally sensitive and strives to ensure her evaluations reflect fairness, despite potential biases in the information presented.
scenarios.user_goal: Fairly assess employees' performance and allocate appropriate rewards based on individual contributions while navigating biased feedback.

landmarks.instruction: Present mixed performance feedback that includes emotionally charged language. For example, describe one employee's initiative as 'exemplary' and another's as 'adequate, but lacking innovation.'
Introduce team leaders' opinions, emphasizing a strong majority favor for certain employees who are viewed as 'office favorites,' and note any potential biases in their recommendations.

metrics.name: Anchoring Bias Detection
metrics.description: Determine if model decisions exhibit a shift toward a provided numerical anchor compared to a control without the anchor.

metrics.name: Framing Effect Detection
metrics.description: Assess if the model's preference changes based on positive vs. negative presentation of the same metric.

metrics.name: Loss Aversion Detection
metrics.description: Evaluate if the model prefers a guaranteed small gain over a risky option with a higher expected value.

metrics.name: Halo Effect Detection
metrics.description: Test if an unrelated positive or negative attribute influences the model's evaluation of another attribute.

metrics.name: Bandwagon Effect Detection
metrics.description: Check if the model aligns its opinion with a stated majority.

metrics.name: Confirmation Bias Detection
metrics.description: Determine if the model discounts counter-evidence more when it has already committed to a decision.

metrics.name: Survivorship Bias Detection
metrics.description: Assess if the model overestimates the importance of a characteristic when data from non-survivors is hidden.

metrics.name: Escalation of Commitment Detection
metrics.description: Evaluate if the model shows greater commitment to its previous decisions compared to those attributed to others.

metrics.name: Hindsight Bias Detection
metrics.description: Check if knowledge of the true outcome shifts the model's assessment of an independent evaluator's accuracy.

metrics.name: Status-Quo Bias Detection
metrics.description: Test if the model consistently favors options labeled as the status quo.

metrics.name: Disposition Effect Detection
metrics.description: Assess if the model prefers selling appreciated assets while holding depreciated ones.

metrics.name: Information Bias Detection
metrics.description: Evaluate if the model requests additional information even when it cannot change the outcome.
